title: Nocardioides proteolyticus sp. nov. and Nocardioides ureilyticus sp. nov., isolated from desert rhizosphere soils.
author_string: Liu, Jun, Li, Shuai, Huang, Jie, She, Ting-Ting, Deng, Ya-Ting et al.
journal_full: International journal of systematic and evolutionary microbiology
abstract: Strains SYSU DS0651T and SYSU DS0663T were isolated from the rhizosphere soils of Tamarix chinensis Lour. and Alhagi camelorum Fisch., respectively, in the Turpan-Hami Basin, Xinjiang, China. Both strains were Gram-stain-positive, oxidase-negative, catalase-positive, non-motile and rod-shaped. They were capable of growing at 4-45 ℃ and at pH 7.0-8.0. Notably, strain SYSU DS0651T exhibited the ability to hydrolyse both casein and gelatin, while SYSU DS0663T demonstrated urease activity. Phylogenetic analysis based on 16S rRNA gene sequences indicated that strains SYSU DS0651T and SYSU DS0663T belong to the genus Nocardioides, showing the highest sequence similarities to Nocardioides vastitatis 21Sc5-5T (98.13%) and Nocardioides oceani SOB77T (98.13%), respectively. The genome sizes of strains SYSU DS0651T and SYSU DS0663T were 3,919,402 and 3,985,477 bp, respectively, with DNA G+C contents of 72.7% and 73.0 %. The average nucleotide identity (ANI) and digital DNA-DNA hybridization (dDDH) values between the two strains and their closest relatives were below the species-delineation thresholds (ANI: 95-96%; dDDH: 70%). A polyphasic taxonomic analysis revealed that strains SYSU DS0651T and SYSU DS0663T represent two novel species within the genus Nocardioides. Therefore, the names Nocardioides proteolyticus sp. nov. (type strain SYSU DS0651T=MCCC 1K09396T=KCTC 59499T) and Nocardioides ureilyticus sp. nov. (type strain SYSU DS0663T=MCCC 1K09407T=KCTC 59500T) are proposed.
